LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES. NOT CONTESTED. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED: DEFENDANT SAM SULEMAN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE SULEMAN DECLARATION DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THE MEANS BY WHICH PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED, AS REQUIRED BY CCP 430.41. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS ARE NOW DUE BY APRIL 10, 2024. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. BAILIFF: PARK. NOT REPORTED. (301/RCE)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-07-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAM SULEMAN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) (COURTESY COPIES). THE DEMURRER MAYBE RE-SET FOR A MONDAY-THURSDAY AFTER MAY 21. ALL PAPERS SHALL BEAR NEW HEARING DATE. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Brian B. Miles, Esq., MILES WESTBROOK & DEAL LLP (925) 938-4500 brian@mileswestbrook.com
Attorney for Plaintiff THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.
SAM SULEMAN, samsuleman@gmail.com Pro Per Defendant

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAM SULEMAN's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. The FAC adequately pleads breach of contract against defendant. The contract attached to the FAC states that it is with "Customer" and identifies defendant as "Customer." Facts can be further developed in discovery.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. Not contested. The tentative ruling is adopted: DEFENDANT SAM SULEMAN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar. The Suleman declaration does not demonstrate the means by which parties met and conferred, as required by CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Responsive pleadings are now due by April 10, 2024. Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Park. Not Reported. (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAM SULEMAN'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment

Pro per defendant's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The motion is well within CCP 473(b)'s six-month window. Confusion over a demurrer is demonstrated and thus "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (Id.) It is the strong policy of California's courts to resolve cases on their merits.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
